zh.Macclesfield St Michael, Prestbury, Ches: 英國爾普雷斯特波利馬可萊斯費爾德 聖 邁克爾
zh.lifeSummary: Baroness Beatrix de Hulme是Bertrand Hulme的女兒，於1208年出生在英國爾切斯特。她於1225年與Baron Vivian de Davenport在英國英格蘭爾切斯特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1250年在英國英格蘭爾享年42歲，埋葬在英國爾普雷斯特波利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hulme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.

variants.name: Hume
meanings: Scottish (Lowlands) and northern English: variant of Hulme , with loss of l before the bilabial consonant /m/, as in the vocabulary word calm. See Holm and Home .

Scottish: habitational name either from the barony of Home or from Hume (or Home) in Berwickshire, from which the barony took its name. The placename derives from Old English hōum, dative plural form of hōh ‘heel, spur of land’.
History: A family of this name (see 2 above) have held the Earldom of Home since 1605.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Baroness Beatrix de Hulme was born in 1208, in Chester, Cheshire, England as the daughter of Bertrand Hulme. She married Baron Vivian de Davenport in 1225, in Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1250, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 42, and was buried in Prestbury, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Baroness Beatrix de Hulme est née en 1208, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, fille de Bertrand Hulme. Elle a épousé Baron Vivian de Davenport en 1225, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1250, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Prestbury, Cheshire, Angleterre.

ko.Macclesfield St Michael, Prestbury, Ches: 영국 체실르 플레스트버이 맥클스필드 스트 믹하얼
ko.lifeSummary: Baroness Beatrix de Hulme(은)는 1208년 영국 체실르 체스터에서 Bertrand Hulme의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터에서 Baron Vivian de Davenport(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 플레스트버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Baroness Beatrix de Hulme родилась в 1208 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия. Её отца звали Bertrand Hulme. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baron Vivian de Davenport в 1225 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1250 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности престбури, чешир, Англия.
